author: data.always
content:
Now that client tagging in graffiti is normalized, I will point out that Binance (the 2nd largest tagged proposer set on Dune, at ~8% of staking market share--probably even larger with better tagging) runs 99% Lighthouse. Not sure if anyone has contacts there, but if someone convinced them to swap CL clients it would drop LH dominance from ~52% to ~44% and the Network would be much healthier. 

https://dune.com/queries/7971900/11955783

author: nflaig
content: can we convince them to run a multi-client setup and use vero or vouch? it seems warranted if you manage that much stake, especially I am also assuming these are user funds they are staking, maybe <@737740104281489531> can preach to them, he's been advocating for years and did a really good job

author: serenita_io
content:
I'd be more than happy to explain to them how this kind of bug – https://x.com/potuz_eth/status/1768418899111113125 – in Lighthouse could lead to them losing about 50% of that delegated staked ETH. That is without getting a single validator slashed.

Running even a relatively simple 2-out-of-3 multi-node setup protects from that. It's much better than just switching to a single different client. More than warranted for an operation of that size.

author: serenita_io
content:
While we're on the topic, here's a proposal for measuring client usage in an arguably better way than today's graffiti, that also accounts for multi-node setups: https://ethresear.ch/t/in-protocol-client-data-reporting/25353

It would allow us to better judge how big the impact of consensus bugs would be.
